name: Roam Research
price_basis: advertised Pro plan billed monthly, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KILL: $180 a year for backlinks between text files. The graph is a folder plus grep.
what_zo_builds: The graph exports as markdown and lands as files. A small indexer the agent writes recomputes the backlinks: every [[reference]] links both ways, rendered by a route with full text search and a daily notes page that creates itself. The outliner keystrokes go away. The thinking stays in files you own.
dont_kill_if: You live in the outliner and block references all day. For some people that workflow is the product, and no file tree fakes block level transclusion well.
build_brief: Goal: replace Roam with markdown and a backlink index. Export my graph as markdown into /notes/import. Write an indexer that scans every [[wiki link]] and builds a backlinks map, then a route at /notes that renders each page with its linked references underneath. Add a daily note that creates itself on first visit each day, and full text search across everything. Done when I can open today's note, link to any page, and see the backlink appear on that page without Roam in the loop.
